revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:瓢箪塚古墳 (海老名市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Hisagozuka Kofun (Ebina), funkyu-1.jpg|location={{ill|Ebina|qid=Q734390|1=Ebina, Kanagawa}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.447916666666664|N|139.40166666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} {{Japanese Kofuns |name = Hisagozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Hisagozuka Kofun (Ebina), funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound (with the front part on the left and the rear circular part on the back right) |alternate_name = Hisagoyama Kofun / Kamihamada No. 7 Kofun | part_of = Kamihamada Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ebina, Kanagawa|en|Ebina, Kanagawa|qid=Q734390|lt=Ebina|1=Ebina, Kanagawa}}, Kokubun Minami 3-Chome 1055 (within Hisagozuka Park) |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 26|coordinates = 52.57 |coordinates = 139|coordinates = 24|coordinates = 6.07 |omitted = JP-14 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |area = Mound length 80m |burial = Unknown |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埵輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |AssociatedMounds = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|qid=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}} – Early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} |BuriedIndividual = |Tomb = |Historic Site = Designated Ebina City Historic Site "Hisagozuka Kofun" |TangibleHistoric Site = |notes = |map = Japan Kanagawa |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - south-southwest |map_dot_label = Hisagozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Hisagozuka Kofun''' (瓶筒坂古坛, Hisagoyama Kofun / Kamihamada No. 7 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古坛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} located in {{ill|Ebina, Kanagawa|en|Ebina, Kanagawa|qid=Q734390|lt=Ebina|1=Ebina, Kanagawa}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and one of the mounds comprising the Kamihamada Kofun Cluster. It has been Historic Site1 a historic site by Ebina City. == Overview == This kofun is situated atop the {{ill|Sagamino plateau|ja|座間丘陵|lt=Zama Hills|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagamino plateau|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相模野台地|1=Sagamino plateau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11582177}}, a part of the {{ill|Sagamino plateau|en|Sagamino plateau|qid=Q11582177|lt=Sagamino plateau|1=Sagamino plateau}}, overlooking the {{ill|Sagami River|en|Sagami River|qid=Q612253|lt=Sagami River|1=Sagami River}} and surrounding plains. The cluster comprises seven Kofuns of various types, including keyhole-shaped, round, and square mounds, with Hisagozuka being the only keyhole-shaped mound among them. An excavation was conducted in 1996.Intypeation board at the site. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front portion facing south. The front edge is now missing. The mound is estimated to measure approximately 80 meters in length, making it the largest Kofun in Ebina City. Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埵輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were discovered on the mound’s surface. The burial facility has not been investigated, so details remain unclear. Hisagozuka Kofun is believed to have been built in the late {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|qid=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}} to early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}. The unearthed haniwa fragments are among the oldest in Kanagawa Prefecture, highlighting the mound’s importance as one of the earliest significant burial sites in the region.{{Cite book|editor=|author=Takeshi Otani|year=1989|chapter=Hisagoyama Kofun|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Kofuns|ja|日本古坛大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607}} The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by Ebina City in 1998 and is now preserved within Hisagozuka Park. == Historical Record == * 1910 (明治 43): Mentioned in "Shrine and Scenic Sites Records." * 1919 (大正 8): Acquired as village-owned land by Ebina Village (now Ebina City). * 1996 (平成 8): Excavation conducted (report published in 1997 by the Ebina City Archaeological Survey Group). * 1998 (平成 10): Designated as an Ebina City Historic Site on August 28. File:Hisagozuka Kofun (Ebina), funcho.jpg|Top of the rear circular mound File:Hisagozuka Kofun (Ebina), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front to the rear circular mound File:Hisagozuka Kofun (Ebina), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear to the front portion File:Hisagozuka Kofun (Ebina), sekihi.jpg|Stone marker in front of the mound’s south-facing entrance == Historic Site == === Ebina City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Hisagozuka Kofun - Designated on August 28, 1998.{{PDFlink|[https://www.city.ebina.kanagawa.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/008/794/ichiranR2.4.pdf List of Designated and Related Historic Site in Ebina City]}} (Ebina City website). == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ebina City Onko-kan|ja|海老名市清故館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebina City Onko-kan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ebina City Museum of Local History, located in Kokubun Minami, Ebina City) - Displays haniwa from Hisagozuka Kofun and burial goods from Kamihamada No. 2 Kofun. == notes == {{notes help}} {{Reflist}} == References == {{small|(Sources used in the article)}} * Site intypeation board (installed by the Ebina City Board of Education, 2001) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Hisagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place name Series|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17213509}} Volume 14 Kanagawa Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|qid=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582910335}} == Related Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly used in the article)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=|title=Hisagozuka Kofun: Excavation Report of Kamihamada Kofun Cluster No. 7|publisher=Ebina City Archaeological Survey Group|isbn=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Akiyama Kofun Cluster|ja|秋葉山古坛群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiyama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579003|1=Akiyama Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Katsusaka Arikaya Site|ja|勝坂有鹿谷遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsusaka Arikaya Site|1=Katsusaka Arukayato Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q110856286}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Hisagozuka Kofun (Ebina)}} * [https://ebina-kankou.com/%E7%93%A2%E7%AE%AA%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-2/ Hisagozuka Kofun] - Ebina City Tourism Website "Ebina Meguri" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|瓢箪塚古墳 (海老名市)|version=99995166|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:瓶筒坂古坛]] [[ja:瓢箪塚古墳 (海老名市)]] [[ja:瓢箪塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Hisagozuka Kofun (Ebina)}} {{wikidata link|Q110814750}} [[Category:Kofun of Kanagawa prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ebina]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Historic Sites in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
